description_long: This is a dark green, single-propeller military floatplane with a streamlined fuselage and elliptical wings, featuring prominent red roundels on the wings and fuselage. The aircraft is equipped with two large floats beneath the wings for water landings, and a smaller tail float for stability. The cockpit is enclosed and positioned just behind the engine, with a clear canopy and visible framing. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, all painted in matching green with red markings. The propeller is mounted at the nose, and the engine cowling is painted black. The overall design is reminiscent of World War II-era Japanese seaplanes, optimized for both flight and aquatic operations.
description: A dark green, single-propeller military floatplane with red roundels, twin wing-mounted floats, and an enclosed cockpit. The design is streamlined for both flight and water landings.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the elliptical wings with red roundels, twin floats, and the streamlined fuselage.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the large wing float, cockpit canopy, and the propeller at the nose.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the propeller, engine cowling, and the symmetrical placement of the floats.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the tail float, cockpit, and the detailed markings on the fuselage and tail.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing monoplane with straight, elliptical wings and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Twin main floats under the wings and a single tail float; floatplane configuration.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a clear canopy just behind the engine.
